content: {"analyses": [{"source_url": "https://www.ipcc.ch/srocc/chapter/chapter-2/", "source_title": "Chapter 2: High Mountain Areas", "source_type": "web", "source_quality": 0.7, "overall_relevance": 0.65, "year": 0, "authors": [], "venue": "", "doi": "", "atomic_facts": [{"statement": "ES Executive Summary The cryosphere (including, snow, glaciers, permafrost, lake and river ice) is an integral element of high mountain regi...", "direct_quote": "ES Executive Summary The cryosphere (including, snow, glaciers, permafrost, lake and river ice) is an integral element of high mountain regions, which are home to roughly 10% of the global population.", "fact_category": "statistic", "relevance_score": 0.65, "confidence": 0.88, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": []}, {"statement": "Snow cover duration has declined in nearly all regions, especially at lower elevations, on average by 5 days per decade, with a likely2 rang...", "direct_quote": "Snow cover duration has declined in nearly all regions, especially at lower elevations, on average by 5 days per decade, with a likely2 range from 0\u201310 days per decade.", "fact_category": "statistic", "relevance_score": 0.65, "confidence": 0.88, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": []}, {"statement": "Mass change of glaciers in all mountain regions (excluding the Canadian and Russian Arctic, Svalbard, Greenland and Antarctica) was very lik...", "direct_quote": "Mass change of glaciers in all mountain regions (excluding the Canadian and Russian Arctic, Svalbard, Greenland and Antarctica) was very likely -490 \u00b1 100 kg m-2 yr-1 (-123 \u00b1 24 Gt yr-1) in 2006\u20132015.", "fact_category": "statistic", "relevance_score": 0.65, "confidence": 0.88, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": []}], "evidence_summary": "Auto-extracted 3 facts."}]}
